articles.title: Alzheimer’s patient gets back speech, continence and memory after 5 grams of experimental drug
articles.description: A patient with advanced Alzheimer’s disease showed remarkable signs of improvement after taking a heroic dose of this experimental hallucinogen.

articles.title: A study of 8,300 older adults revealed a surprising salt habit
articles.description: A large study of older adults in Brazil found that adding extra salt at the table is still a common habit, especially among men. While too much salt is linked to serious health problems and faster cognitive decline, researchers discovered that women’s salt-shaking habits were tied more closely to lifestyle and diet.

articles.title: Tea has a positive effect on biological aging – and this is the amount you should drink daily
articles.description: A study has shown that drinking a sufficient amount of tea every day can do wonders for health – and even slow down biological aging, improve memory, and assist in weight loss.

articles.title: Irregular sleep patterns reduce cognitive performance in preschool children
articles.description: A new study to be presented at the SLEEP 2026 annual meeting found that irregular sleep - including variability in sleep timing and duration - was associated with lower receptive vocabulary and visuospatial memory scores in preschool-age children, even after accounting for total sleep duration.

articles.title: Egypt arrest fake heart surgeon for running clinic using forged credentials
articles.description: Man posing as heart surgeon and professor arrested in Egypt for running clinic on forged documents, as authorities and medical bodies tighten credential checks.

articles.title: Hartford-Area Hospital Earns Distinction For Diabetes Education
articles.description: The hospital and affiliated clinics earned American Diabetes Association recognition for diabetes education and support services.

articles.title: Cramer’s Stop Trading: FedEx Freight Holding Company
articles.description: CNBC’s Jim Cramer explains why he is keeping an eye on shares of FedEx Freight Holding Company.

articles.title: NMDP Celebrates Milestone 150,000th Transplant Representing Nearly Four Decades of Hope, Innovation and Second Chances
articles.description: MINNEAPOLIS, June 04, 2026 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- NMDPSM, the global nonprofit leader in cell therapy, today celebrates its 150,000 th transplant facilitated since its founding in 1987. This landmark milestone represents decades of scientific innovation, growth of the world's most diverse registry of volunteer blood stem cell donors and an unwavering commitment to patients in need of a second chance at life. The announcement comes as NMDP continues accelerating access to transplant for patients facing blood cancers and disorders such as leukemia, lymphoma, sickle cell disease, and other life-threatening diseases. "This moment belongs to every patient, every donor, every caregiver, every volunteer, every transplant center partner and every supporter who believed we could lean into innovation and community generosity to offer more patients treatment through blood stem cell transplant," said Amy Ronneberg, CEO of NMDP. "Behind 150,000 transplants are 150,000 human stories of families given more time together to create more memories made possible because someone said yes to helping a stranger. It is both a profound moment of gratitude and a powerful reminder that our work is far from finished." A Media Snippet accompanying this announcement is available by clicking on this link. Every movement starts somewhere. NMDP began with a little girl named Laura Graves. In 1979, 10-year-old Graves received ... Full story available on Benzinga.com

articles.title: Chai Discovery Announces License Agreement with Pfizer to Accelerate Drug Discovery with AI
articles.description: SAN FRANCISCO--(BUSINESS WIRE)--Chai Discovery, the company engineering AI models to discover new molecules, announces a license agreement with Pfizer.

articles.title: Teen insomnia symptoms linked to hospitalizations in young adulthood
articles.description: A new study to be presented at the SLEEP 2026 annual meeting found that poorer sleep health according to wrist actigraphy and insomnia symptoms during adolescence predicted a higher likelihood of an overnight hospital stay during young adulthood around seven years later, even after accounting for adolescent hospitalizations and sleep health.

articles.description: The CQC report revealed there were ‘extended periods without rest’ for some healthcare professionals

However, even if a product includes it, the amount might not be sufficient to make a meaningful impact. That’s why we’re happy we found this niacinamide serum that seriously brightens, clears and smooths [...]

articles.title: A murder baffled investigators for decades. Then a Georgia man found religion
articles.description: More than three decades after a New Jersey killing, a Georgia resident confessed, saying his faith led him to come forward.

articles.title: RFK Jr. seeks to peek at Americans’ medical records for clues on autism and vaccines
articles.description: U.S. health secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. is pursuing federal government access to most Americans’ medical records, in a quest to research a link between vaccines and autism — a connection the medical establishment studied for decades and flatly rejects.

articles.title: How packaging makes us think food is healthy – when the opposite is true
articles.description: Studies reveal how colors, captions and markings on food packaging cause us to perceive products as healthy – even when they are loaded with sugar, salt and fat.

articles.title: EastEnders’ Denise issued serious warning as she takes a turn for the worse in cancer fight
articles.description: Denise Fox continues to bury her head in the sand over her blood cancer diagnosis in the BBC soap next week.

articles.title: 'Memory stocks are the star of the show'; Goldman's Kospi 12,000 call goes beyond the memory trade
articles.description: Goldman Sachs' Timothy Moe argues memory stocks are positioned to sustain elevated profitability as surging AI-driven compute demand collides with persistent supply constraints, bolstering pricing power across the sector. He also explains why Taiwan could be the next standout Asian market after South Korea, with AI remaining a key driver of the investment case.

articles.title: Need European alternatives to U.S. AI rules, says MEP
articles.description: The EU has unveiled its tech sovereignty package which if adopted, could direct public money towards products that contribute to European independence, cut red tape for data centers and boost research and innovation. Brando Benifei, member of the European Parliament and co-rapporteur on the AI Act joins to discuss.

articles.title: DAMAC says seeking "more than six to eight figures" in loans for data centers
articles.description: DAMAC Chairman Hussain Sajwani says the group is positioning for continued growth in AI-led data centre demand, with 6,000 MW of land bank already in play, and plans to seek a “large amount” of bank borrowing to fund further buildout.

articles.title: ‘Buying stuff like it’s going out of fashion’: Biotech M&A on track for best year since pre-Covid
articles.description: Driven by looming patent cliffs, newly buoyant public markets, and Big Pharma's race to beef up their pipelines, dealmaking in 2026 is off to a strong start.

articles.title: Panda bonds gain traction as investors seek diversified, stable Yuan assets: AllianceBernstein
articles.description: Eric Liu from AllianceBernstein explans why there is growing interest in China's onshore bonds.

articles.title: Cancer’s favorite escape trick may actually make it easier to kill
articles.description: Scientists have uncovered a surprising new way the immune system fights cancer, overturning a core belief that has guided immunology for decades. The research found that when cancer cells shut down a key immune-recognition molecule called MHC I—a common trick used to hide from “killer” T cells—they can actually become more vulnerable to attack by a different group of immune cells known as CD4+ “helper” T cells.

articles.title: Norway’s Princess Ingrid Alexandra leaves school in Australia to be with ‘seriously ill’ mom
articles.description: “The Crown Princess is seriously ill, and I think she has gotten a bit worse lately."

articles.title: Long-term exposure to air pollution associated with lower semantic memory
articles.description: A new study by researchers at UC Davis Health and Kaiser Permanente found that higher exposure to very small air pollution particles (PM2.5) over a 17-year span was associated with lower semantic memory.

articles.title: Clean drinking water shortages threaten food security across nations
articles.description: A new global study has found that people without access to clean drinking water are significantly more likely to experience food insecurity and food safety threats, underscoring the urgent need for coordinated global action to address these issues together.

articles.title: Several Asian economies have AI export boom to offset the hit from energy crisis: Economist
articles.description: Paul Gruenwald from S&P Global Ratings discusses Asia's economic outlook amid AI boom and energy shock.

articles.title: Light-activated proteins demonstrate quantum sensing and radio wave control
articles.description: Until now, quantum sensing has primarily been known from solid-state materials such as diamonds with deliberately introduced tiny defects.

The sleep habit you think is healthy but is actually an ‘ominous sign’
articles.description: This habit may suggest you're a 'good sleeper' but it might be a sign that your body is carrying sleep debt and is struggling to stay awake.

articles.title: What pregnancy reveals about a woman’s future heart health
articles.description: Pregnancy acts as a natural cardiovascular stress test, revealing hidden heart disease and identifying women at higher long-term risk after complications such as preeclampsia, gestational diabetes, and preterm birth. The review highlights major evidence gaps in cardio-obstetric care and calls for stronger postpartum screening, dedicated women’s health clinics, and better inclusion of reproductive-age women in research.

articles.title: New warnings over controversial supplement Kratom
articles.description: Concern is growing over a controversial supplement called Kratom sold legally across most of the country. The CDC warns it's leading to an increase in poison control calls. NBC News' Steven Romo speaks to one woman who sought professional help to quit.

articles.title: Women Over 30 Shared Their Honest And Vulnerable Reasons Why They Started Taking GLP-1 Medication
articles.description: "I was 405 pounds and in the hospital after a bout with COVID. I had diastolic heart failure and lung complications. One of four cardiologists standing in my room crossed his arms and came close to me, saying, 'You...will either change your life or live in a nursing home.'" View Entire Post ›

articles.title: Could GLP-1 drugs help prevent breast cancer? New study points to a possible link
articles.description: A large retrospective cohort study of 111,646 overweight or obese women found that GLP-1 agonist exposure was associated with lower breast cancer incidence. After propensity-score matching, GLP-1 exposure was linked to nearly 30% lower odds of breast cancer, but the authors stress that randomized trials are needed before these drugs can be considered for prevention.

articles.title: Free summer yoga and Pilates classes coming to Ann Arbor’s Broadway Park West
articles.description: Broadway Park West will host free outdoor fitness classes along the Huron River this summer as part of a new wellness program to bring the community together.

articles.title: Infertility or endometriosis could be linked to early menopause risk: study
articles.description: A study published by the Menopause Society has found that women who had unexplained infertility or endometriosis had an increased risk of early menopause happening before age 45.

articles.title: What we still get wrong about how people from non-Western backgrounds recover from trauma
articles.description: Western PTSD treatments typically focus on talking to a therapist about your feelings and emotions. But this doesn’t actually help everyone.
